title: Ethanol precipitation of nucleic acids (96-well plate)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4hhpe","text":"Nucleic acid precipitation is used to concentrate and/or purify nucleic acids. The below protocol is based on the fact that nucleic acids are less soluble in alcohol than in more polar water. Addition of salt further decreases solubility by competing for water dipoles; as does low temperature. Please see the OpenWetWare website for more details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":310,"length":11}],"data":[]},{"key":"99r1f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ga42","text":"Citation: OpenWetWare contributors, 'Ethanol precipitation of nucleic acids', OpenWetWare, , 1 July 2012, 10:48 UTC, \u003chttp://openwetware.org/index.php?title=Ethanol_precipitation_of_nucleic_acids\u0026oldid=611420\u003e [accessed 21 January 2015]","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":78,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":118,"length":90}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"1D4CF851747444A2828F8FC6834BEAB1","url":"http://openwetware.org/wiki/Ethanol_precipitation_of_nucleic_acids"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4F1F5E043AC34A2CB1234491EC3379D3","url":"http://openwetware.org/index.php?title=Ethanol_precipitation_of_nucleic_acids\u0026oldid=611420"}}}}
steps: Add to each 10 µl product: 1.9 µl of Na acetate 3M undetermined Sodium acetate P212121
Add to each 10 µl product: 60 µl of 85% ethanol undetermined
Mix thoroughly (vortex ???) and keep at -20°C for 30 min 00:30:00
centrifuge for 45 min at 4000 rpm and 4°C 00:45:00
remove supernatant (invert tube on trash once)
add 150 µl of 70% ethanol and mix undetermined
centrifuge for 15 min at 4000 rpm and 4°C 00:15:00
remove all supernatant (invert tube on trash)
take out the tube and let it dry in the fume hood at room temperature for 10-15 min 00:10:00
put 20 µl of formide dye using multipipette (nasty chemical to manipulate in fume hood) undetermined
transfer the 20 µl (multipipette) in a sequencing plate
put septum on top, press, tap once (do NOT mix)
keep on ice in aluminium rack
Heat 3 min at 95°C (SWATI/95-CST) to keep in single stranded form 00:03:00
keep on ice in aluminium rack
creator: Openwetware
